title: Winning back the pizza buyer
footer: CASE STUDY: PIZZA HUT MARKETING STRATEGY

card.eyebrow: Case study · Pizza Hut marketing strategy
card.title: Winning back the pizza buyer
card.dek: A state-by-state read on where Pizza Hut should defend dining rooms versus run delivery-only ghost kitchens.

blocks.title: Winning back the pizza buyer
blocks.dek: A high-intent audience for Pizza Hut, and a state-by-state read on where to keep dining rooms versus where to run delivery-only kitchens.

blocks.title: The audience
blocks.lead: The widest credible pool of high-intent buyers worth re-engaging: everyone is in-market for pizza, crossed with the behaviors that actually move reach.

blocks.title: Composition
blocks.note: Curbside pickup, very-high-spend families, casual dining and price sensitivity were dropped: each added under 5% new reach because they already sat inside the bigger signals.

blocks.title: Where to build what
blocks.lead: Delivery intent outweighs dine-in roughly **4 to 1** nationally, so the map reads *relative* skew. The index is a state's dine-in share ÷ its delivery share × 100: above 100 → dine-in over-indexes (favor brick-and-mortar); below 100 → delivery over-indexes (favor ghost kitchens).

blocks.title: Market playbook
blocks.lead: How the index translates into footprint decisions, by state cluster.

items.label: Lead with dine-in
items.desc: Keep and refresh physical restaurants: the sit-down “Hut” experience over-indexes here.

items.label: Hybrid footprint
items.desc: Mix dine-in and delivery: neither channel dominates among pizza households.

items.label: Lead with ghost kitchens
items.desc: Favor lower-capex delivery-only kitchens over full dining-room builds.

items.body: The audience is anchored on deal-seeking and coupon behavior: promotional, bundle-led offers are the lever that reactivates these households.
Defend and modernize dining rooms in the dine-in states; convert or open delivery-only kitchens in the ghost-kitchen states to cut occupancy cost where demand is already digital.

items.lead: Treat carryout as a margin play.
items.body: It’s small and precise in the data: an underserved, high-margin channel to grow in hybrid and dine-in markets alike.
A tighter high-intent cut can prove ROI in one region before rolling the full 13.2M audience out on Meta or Google.

blocks.lead: Read the map as format mix, not presence.
blocks.body: Texas and California index toward delivery but are enormous absolute markets: the skew should shape the dine-in-vs-ghost-kitchen ratio there, not whether Pizza Hut shows up at all.

blocks.text: METHOD: Audience and channel splits measured on the Watt Signal Graph (US adults, refreshed Jun 2026). The siting index compares the state distribution of two deterministic 200K samples, pizza × casual-dining intent vs pizza × food-delivery intent, conditioned on pizza interest. States without enough sample in both audiences are left uncolored. Index = (dine-in share ÷ delivery share) × 100.
